notes.text: First comprehensive blood panel. Vitamin D quite low — starting 4000 IU/day supplementation. Fasting 14 hours before draw.
Summer recheck. Vitamin D improving but still below optimal. Added magnesium glycinate 400mg before bed. Noticed increased thirst and urination — worth monitoring glucose.
Glucose and insulin continuing to climb. Reduced refined carbs and started zone 2 cardio 3x/week. Vitamin D finally in good range.
HOMA-IR now above reference range. Doctor discussed potential pre-diabetic trajectory. Testosterone still declining. Starting creatine 5g/day and B-complex for homocysteine.

diagnoses.note: Family history: Type 2 diabetes (father, diagnosed at 55), hypertension (paternal grandmother). Occasional lower back pain from desk work. No chronic medications.

diet.lunch: Grilled chicken or fish with large salad, olive oil, legumes
diet.dinner: Steak or salmon with roasted vegetables and sweet potato
diet.snacks: Mixed nuts, Greek yogurt with berries, dark chocolate
diet.note: Intermittent fasting — skips breakfast. High protein (~150g/day). Reduced refined carbs since July 2025. 2-3 coffees/day. Social alcohol 1-2 drinks on weekends.

exercise.note: Strength training 3x/week (push-pull-legs, progressive overload). Zone 2 cycling added Nov 2025 (30-45 min, 3x/week). Daily step goal 8000+. Uses standing desk intermittently.

sleepRest.note: Usually 23:00-06:30. Blue light glasses after sunset. Some difficulty falling asleep on training days. Previously worked rotating shifts (2020-2023) — may have residual circadian disruption.

lightCircadian.note: Software developer — heavy screen time. Morning sunlight walk 10-15 min. Works from home most days.

stress.note: Startup pressure with tight deadlines. Concerned about metabolic trajectory after seeing HOMA-IR trends. Exercise is primary stress relief.

loveLife.note: Married 5 years. Overall good relationship. Noticed slight decline in drive over past year — correlates with testosterone trend.

environment.note: New development suburb. Good air quality area. Recently switched from non-stick to cast iron cookware.

interpretiveLens: Interested in longevity and metabolic health optimization. Follows a metabolic health framework focused on early insulin resistance detection. Family history of T2D. Values functional/optimal ranges over just reference ranges.
contextNotes: Data-driven and tracks metrics closely. Wants to understand the relationship between declining testosterone, rising insulin, and lifestyle factors. Prefers evidence-based interventions.

healthGoals.text: Reverse insulin resistance trend — get HOMA-IR back below 2.0
Support testosterone levels naturally through lifestyle
Maintain vitamin D between 100-150 nmol/L year-round

assessments.label: Initial assessment

rooms.name: Bedroom

rfMicrowave.meter: Safe Living Technologies Safe and Sound Pro II

rooms.note: High AC electric from ungrounded Romex in walls. WiFi router in adjacent room. Smart meter on exterior wall directly below bedroom.

rooms.name: Home Office

rfMicrowave.meter: Safe Living Technologies Safe and Sound Pro II

rooms.note: Triple monitor setup, WiFi router in same room, LED desk lamp with noisy driver. Already using wired ethernet for desktop.

rooms.name: Living Room

rfMicrowave.meter: Safe Living Technologies Safe and Sound Pro II

rooms.note: Lower electric fields on ground floor. RF elevated from neighbor's WiFi and nearby cell tower.

assessments.note: New construction home (2021). Standard Romex wiring without grounded metal conduit. Smart meter installed by utility. Consultant recommended demand switch for bedroom circuit and relocating WiFi router.
assessments.interpretation:
## Office is the lever

- **Office desk AC electric field** measured high — likely ungrounded desk lamp or PC tower. Drives 8h/day during work.
  - **Mitigation:** ground the desk circuit, replace ungrounded LED desk lamps with grounded units, route monitor + tower cables behind a metal shield, work with grounded mat/keyboard.
- **Bedroom WiFi router** at 1.5 m head distance is too close — relocate + hardwire critical devices. Alternative: WiFi timer 7 am–10 pm only.
- **Living-area dirty electricity** moderate — Stetzer/Greenwave filters on heaviest-load circuits.

Lower priority:
- Bedroom magnetic field: low, no action needed.
- Cellular signal weak (≈ –100 dBm); phone increases its own output to compensate. Speakerphone + airplane mode at night.

assessments.label: Post-remediation

rooms.name: Bedroom

rfMicrowave.meter: Safe Living Technologies Safe and Sound Pro II

rooms.note: Demand switch installed on bedroom circuit — AC electric now in green range. WiFi router relocated to ground floor. Stetzerizer filters on bedroom outlets. Smart meter RF still detectable but reduced.

rooms.name: Home Office

rfMicrowave.meter: Safe Living Technologies Safe and Sound Pro II

rooms.note: Replaced LED desk lamp with incandescent. Shielded monitor cables. Stetzerizer filters on outlet strip. WiFi now downstairs — using wired only in office.

rooms.name: Living Room

rfMicrowave.meter: Safe Living Technologies Safe and Sound Pro II

rooms.note: WiFi router now in this room (relocated from upstairs). RF from external cell tower unchanged.

assessments.note: Follow-up after remediation. Bedroom dramatically improved — demand switch was the biggest win. Office improved with Stetzerizer filters and cable shielding. Living room RF from cell tower remains — shielding paint option discussed for future.

aiAnalysis.tip: Setup complete — III skin, Prague, CZ, Ott score 5/10
aiAnalysis.detail: Skin type III drives the burn-threshold + vit-D yield curves. Prague (~50°N) means winter sun is too low for vit-D synthesis Nov-Feb; cap session length aggressively in summer noon. Ott score 5/10 suggests room for evening light-burden reduction.

lightDevices.notes: Anchor device — calibrated against dminder app. Use full mode 5×/wk midday.
Bedside — used post-workout for recovery + on workdays as eye-channel anchor.

aiAnalysis.tip: Solid 30-min session, well within safe band
aiAnalysis.detail: 35% body fraction at UVI 5.8. Vit-D synthesis on track, 30% MED leaves room for one more session today if you want.

aiAnalysis.tip: Face-only exposure — circadian + eye good, vit-D minimal
aiAnalysis.detail: ~5% of skin exposed. Strong morning circadian + violet-eye signals at the eye, but vit-D synthesis stays low under 10% body fraction. If vit-D is a goal, a single mid-day arms-out session covers more ground than four face-only ones.

rooms.name: Bedroom
rooms.notes: Switched to amber bulbs for evening + blackout curtains installed mid-March.

aiAnalysis.tip: Warm bedroom light + evening use — good evening config
aiAnalysis.detail: 2700K amber bulbs + 1-3hr of post-sunset use. Melanopic load stays low, sleep onset uninterrupted.

rooms.name: Office
rooms.notes: Window-side desk; natural daylight 9am–4pm. Overhead LED only on cloudy days.

aiAnalysis.tip: Cool-white workspace, daytime-only — appropriate for office
aiAnalysis.detail: 5000K + 7h/day during productive hours feeds the daytime alertness signal. Window-adjacent is even better — natural light dominates 9am-4pm.

aiAnalysis.tip: 3 h/day — light-burden moderate
aiAnalysis.detail: Mixed/unknown lighting in a low-occupancy room is a low-impact lever. Focus on bedroom + workspace first.

aiAnalysis.tip: Blue blocker + minimal evening use — well managed
aiAnalysis.detail: laptop with active blue-light filter + <1hr after sunset. This is the ideal screen profile.

aiAnalysis.tip: Unblocked phone + 1-3hr after sunset
aiAnalysis.detail: Phone/tablet display peaks at ~440nm — same wavelength your circadian system reads as "morning sun". A schedule-based blue-blocker (Night Shift / f.lux) cuts the melatonin-suppression effect ~40% without changing your usage habits.

burdenAI.tip: Indoor light load is moderate — bedroom evening use is the main lever.
burdenAI.detail: Daytime exposure is good (window-side office, kitchen mixed). Evening bedroom is the weak spot: 1–3hr screen + warm-LED is ok for sleep but 1+ unblocked hr of phone reduces melatonin onset by ~30 min on average. Consider blue-blockers on phone after 9pm OR cutting evening phone use to <1hr.

lightAudits.label: Baseline — pre-bedroom upgrade
lightAudits.notes: Before swapping bedroom bulbs to amber + installing blackout curtains.

rooms.name: Office
rooms.notes: Window-side desk; natural daylight 9am–4pm. Overhead LED only on cloudy days.

aiAnalysis.tip: Cool-white bedroom overhead is the main melatonin suppressor.
aiAnalysis.detail: 4000K bedroom lighting at evening use breaks the sleep-onset signal. Pair this with 1–3hr unblocked phone time and you get measurable phase delay. Highest-leverage fix is bulb swap to <2700K + blackout for the sleep window.

lightAudits.label: After — amber bulbs + blackout
lightAudits.notes: Swapped bedroom to 2700K amber + blackout curtains for sleep window.

rooms.name: Bedroom
rooms.notes: Switched to amber bulbs for evening + blackout curtains installed mid-March.

rooms.name: Office
rooms.notes: Window-side desk; natural daylight 9am–4pm. Overhead LED only on cloudy days.

aiAnalysis.tip: Bedroom melatonin signal restored. Evening phone is now the next lever.
aiAnalysis.detail: Amber bedroom lighting + blackout brings the bedroom into the green band for melanopic-EDI. Remaining lever is evening phone use (1–3hr unblocked) — adding a screen-level blue-block schedule would close the loop.

aiAnalysis.tip: 320 lux — daytime-low for an office
aiAnalysis.detail: Reasonable but below the 500-lux office baseline. Adding window-side time or a daytime-bright lamp would help alertness.

aiAnalysis.tip: 18000 lux — strong daytime signal
aiAnalysis.detail: Plenty of light for the circadian pathway during productive hours.

aiAnalysis.tip: 6 lux — sleep-spec dark
aiAnalysis.detail: Below 50 lux is the ICNIRP-friendly sleep range. Melatonin uninhibited.

aiAnalysis.tip: 2700K — warm, evening-appropriate
aiAnalysis.detail: Below 3000K means low blue content. Good for sleep prep.

aiAnalysis.tip: 4900K — neutral, room-by-room call
aiAnalysis.detail: Mid-CCT works for daytime task lighting; in evening rooms it accelerates melatonin suppression.

aiAnalysis.tip: Flicker score 1 — mild
aiAnalysis.detail: Detected at 240 Hz. Below 200 Hz is correlated with eye strain + headache susceptibility in flicker-sensitive subjects (Wilkins 2018).

aiAnalysis.tip: Warm — incandescent-like
aiAnalysis.detail: Warm-spectrum lighting in evening rooms minimizes melanopic load.

lightMeasurements.notes: Mean 0.2 lux post-blackout, brief 1.4 peak from streetlight

aiAnalysis.tip: Sleep-spec ✓ mean 0.2 lux at the pillow
aiAnalysis.detail: Sleep darkness goal is <1 lux mean. Brief streetlight peaks (max 1.4 lux) are tolerable; constant exposure isn't.

channelMixAI.tip: Strong red-light + morning circadian; vitamin-D channel a bit light
channelMixAI.detail: Weekly mix shows good red-light therapy coverage and morning circadian signal. Vitamin D channel sits at ~70% of weekly target — one more 25-min midday outdoor session would close the gap. Near-infrared from solar tracks your outdoor time; cellular-repair channel is well covered. Mood and outdoor-eye channels good.

focusCard.text: HOMA-IR 0.85 (Feb 2026) cleared the < 2.0 goal — down from 1.40 in Sep — and fasting insulin + HbA1c track the same direction, which is exactly the shape an 8-week zone-2 cardio run + reduced refined carbs should produce. Homocysteine 9.2 µmol/L is also below your < 10 goal six months into the B-complex, so both metabolic arcs are coherent right now. Free testosterone has drifted ~4% over the year (68.2 → 65.4 nmol/L) — within normal age-related variance, but worth re-checking on the next panel before treating it as a trend.

summaries.diagnoses: Insulin resistance reversed; family T2D history
summaries.exercise: Strength + zone-2 cardio drove HOMA-IR drop
summaries.environment: Smart meter + WiFi + RF density elevated
